A beryllium degrader of variable thickness used.
The fluxes were normalized as SUM(F(Ei)*dE)=10**5 .
Flux distributions obtained with SAND-II code:
The aluminum sample (99.999% purity ) consisted of
two parts, each 1 x 1 x 0.5 cm thick, between which
a set of 12 flux monitor foils was inserted.
Irradiation were done for 5 to 20 hr.
Tritium activities induced in Al samples were separated
after addition of H2 carrier by vacuum extraction at
900 degC and were counted in a proportional gas
counting tybe in an anticoincidence arrangement .
The shapes of the neutron spectra were determined by
spectrum unfolding, using the known excitation
functions of the monitor nuclides and their measured
activities.  In a second calculational step, the
excitation function for the triton emission process
on Al-27 was obtained from the neutron flux
distributions and the measured tritium activities.
